summary: Top 6 high-probability picks (all >50% win prob) · NBA Playoffs (BOS-PHI · NYK-ATL · DEN-MIN)

picks.reasoning: The headline pick: only RI line on the slate that's both >50% to hit AND positive EV. Maxey median = 6.9 assists with σ ≈ 3.45 (p75 = 9.2). At -106 implied prob is 51.5% but the model puts true probability at 54.1%, generating +5.1% EV. The -106 juice is unusually thin for a line below the player's median — most books charge -130 or worse on this number. With Embiid drawing doubles and PHI playing from behind, Maxey's pickup-and-roll burden stays high.

Highest hit rate on the board (56.7%). KAT median = 18.9 pts with σ ≈ 8.25, so 17.5 sits comfortably below median. RI's -142 implies 58.7% probability — slightly above the model's 56.7%, so this is a -3.4% EV play (the vig is the cost of safety). Effectively betting that KAT clears his usual 18-20 pt floor, which he should as the Knicks' #1 scoring big. If you want a pick that just gets to the finish line, this is it.

Brown's median = 26.0 pts with σ ≈ 9.6. RI's -118 implies 54.1%; model puts true at 52.1% → -3.7% EV (slight vig drag). This is essentially Brown clearing his per-game scoring average against Philly. With Tatum drawing extra defensive attention from Embiid and George, Brown gets clean looks on the wing all night.

Right at the median — coin-flip city. Embiid p50 = 27.6, σ ≈ 11.27 (the widest distribution on the slate). RI's -110 implies 52.4%; model says 50.4% → -3.8% EV. Just clearing the line at near-even price. The wide variance hurts hit rate (could be a 35-pt explosion or a foul-trouble dud), but the projection sits exactly on the number.

Tatum p50 = 25.0 pts with σ ≈ 9.56 — the line of 24.5 is parked right under median. RI's -118 implies 54.1%; model puts true at 52.0% → -4.0% EV. Effectively a small vig tax for a pick that clears 60% of the time when Tatum plays heavy minutes. BOS is a 6.5-pt road favorite which should keep him on the floor for a normal workload.

Jokic's standard assist line — p50 = 9.9, σ ≈ 3.91. RI's -132 implies 56.9%; model puts true at 54.5% → -4.2% EV. He's elite-stable in this market and almost never goes 7-or-fewer when healthy and on the floor 35+ minutes. The vig is the cost; the floor is rock-solid in a 225-pt projected pace-up game.
